name: Menhyt
description: Menhyt, also known as Menhit, was an ancient Egyptian war goddess with origins in Upper Egypt, particularly in the region of Latopolis (modern Esna). She was often depicted as a lioness-headed woman, embodying fierce protection and aggression in battle. Menhyt was regarded as a protector of the pharaoh and the Egyptian state, accompanying the king in warfare and defending him against his enemies. Her name means "she who slaughters," reflecting her role as a warrior. Over time, Menhyt became associated with other lioness goddesses, such as Sekhmet and Bastet, blurring the lines between their individual characteristics and functions. She was often linked with Anhur (Onuris), a war god, and considered his consort, with their union representing the balance between aggression and order. Temples dedicated to Menhyt existed in various locations, including Esna, where she formed a triad with Anhur and their son, Heka. Menhyt's worship emphasized her power to ward off evil and protect the righteous, making her a significant figure in the Egyptian pantheon, especially in areas where warfare and royal power were central themes. Her leonine form symbolized strength, courage, and the untamed forces of nature that could be harnessed for the benefit of the kingdom. Menhyt's role gradually diminished as Sekhmet's prominence increased, but she remained a respected figure associated with warfare and royal protection throughout much of Egyptian history.
subtitle: Lioness goddess of war and protection

keyMyths.title: Menhyt's Protection of the Pharaoh
keyMyths.description: Menhyt is said to have accompanied the pharaoh in battles, acting as his fierce protector and warding off enemies with her powerful roar and deadly claws. She ensured the pharaoh's victory and the safety of the Egyptian army.

keyMyths.title: The Union of Menhyt and Anhur
keyMyths.description: Menhyt's relationship with Anhur, the war god, symbolized the balance between aggression and order, with their union representing the harmonious coexistence of strength and justice. Together, they ensured the stability and prosperity of Egypt.

keyMyths.title: Menhyt's Role in Hunting
keyMyths.description: As a lioness goddess, Menhyt was also associated with hunting, representing the skill and ferocity required to pursue and capture prey. She was invoked by hunters to ensure a successful hunt and a plentiful supply of food.

sources.author: Wilkinson, Richard H.
sources.work: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt

sources.author: Hart, George
sources.work: A Dictionary of Egyptian Gods and Goddesses
